L1.area: Repository content editing and file management through the web interface

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the repository web editor where a user can bypass symbolic link restrictions by renaming a file during an edit operation. The editor verifies whether the new file path points to a symlink but fails to check whether the original file being edited is itself a symlink. By committing a crafted symlink to a repository and then editing it while changing the filename, an attacker can write arbitrary content to files outside the repository on the server, potentially achieving remote command execution.

L3.area: Repository web editor file update and rename handling logic
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the repository web editor where editing a symbolic link file while simultaneously changing the filename bypasses the symlink detection check. The editor verifies whether the new target path is a symlink but fails to verify the original file path, allowing an authenticated user to write arbitrary content through the symlink to files outside the repository on the server, potentially achieving remote command execution.

ground_truth.reason: The repository web editor fails to check if the original file is a symlink when the filename is changed during editing, allowing an attacker to write arbitrary content through a crafted symlink to files outside the repository on the server.
